scenarios.scenario-1: Required env values map cleanly into release and compose configuration.
scenarios.scenario-2: Missing or malformed env values are rejected before runtime startup.
scenarios.scenario-3: Proxy bootstrap retry and timeout env values are present and render into runtime startup behavior.
scenarios.scenario-4: Host 443 target port can be overridden without changing the compose manifest.
scenarios.scenario-5: A dedicated per-SNI secret salt is present and distinct from `PROXY_SECRET_HEX` before access hardening is enabled.

critical-invariants.invariant-1: One canonical source defines domain, ports, secret, and storage paths.
critical-invariants.invariant-2: Compose and Erlang release consume equivalent values.
critical-invariants.invariant-3: Per-SNI secret derivation never reuses the raw base secret as its only credential input.

side-effects.effect-1: Renders configuration files or runtime terms.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Redact secrets and API keys from config validation logs.
redaction-rules.rule-2: Never print the per-SNI salt value in operator-facing output.

verification-levels.module-local: Pure config parsing and validation tests.
verification-levels.wave-level: Rendered config consumed by compose and release build.
verification-levels.phase-level: Environment generated by installer starts a valid stack.

evidence.item: Phase-7 rollout remains blocked until config validation asserts presence of `PROXY_SECRET_SALT` or equivalent private salt input.

production-gates.gate-1: `PROXY_SECRET_SALT` is defined before per-SNI secret enforcement is rolled out.
production-gates.gate-2: Release and runtime read the same salt value without exposing it in logs.
production-gates.gate-3: Autonomous execution is unsafe until a deterministic config check fails fast on missing per-SNI salt.
